25 Glen Shian Lane · Mt Eliza

A home already resolved. In a place that asks how you want your days to feel.

Land secured, design resolved, planning approved and a fixed-price pathway to construction - within an established Mt Eliza landscape.

Canadian Bay · photo Olivia George

There are few opportunities to create a home in Mt Eliza without first surrendering years to the complexity of land, design, planning, approvals and construction. At 25 Glen Shian Lane, Latitude 37 has already resolved the most demanding parts of that journey. The land is secured. The design is resolved. Planning approval has been obtained. And a fixed-price pathway to construction is in place.

It is an opportunity to move directly into the delivery of a Latitude 37 home within an established Mt Eliza landscape - without the uncertainty, delay and decision fatigue that usually come first.

The result is a rare level of resolution: single-storey living, generous landscaped sites, retained mature vegetation, premium inclusions and a clear, fixed-price route to build.

It is the kind of opportunity many families imagine finding one day - a beautifully resolved home, ready to build, in a place where life can feel more grounded, connected and unhurried. To understand its appeal, it helps to first understand Mt Eliza itself.

The Residences

Two distinct homes, each resolved to the same rare standard.

Home I

Grove House

Shaped by established canopy, layered planting and a sense of retreat. A home that settles quietly into its garden, where privacy and shade give every room a calm, grounded character.

4 bed 3 bath 2 car + rumpus
Canopy · Retreat · Layered planting
Home II

Pavilion House

Shaped by openness, light and seamless indoor-outdoor connection. A home that opens generously to its site, where living space and landscape flow together without seam.

4 bed 3 bath 2 car + garage
Light · Openness · Indoor-outdoor
Planning approved Single-storey living Generous landscaped sites Retained mature vegetation Premium inclusions Fixed-price pathway

There comes a point where families stop asking how close they are to the city.

And start asking how they want their days to feel.

Less compressed. Less scheduled. Less spent in traffic, queues and constant movement. More connected. More outdoors. More present.

Not removed from life. Just closer to the parts that matter.

Most people drive straight past Mt Eliza. That's part of its appeal.

On summer weekends, the Peninsula fills with people heading further south - Mornington, Sorrento, Portsea. But Mt Eliza remains strangely untouched by all of it.

You can still find a car park in the village. Still get a table. Still walk onto a beach and find almost nobody there except the people who live nearby. Locals tend to prefer it that way.

Life here happens around small rituals.

Morning swimsOpen water gatherings at Canadian Bay, as the sun lifts over the water.
Dogs at sunsetThe beach at dusk, familiar faces, the same easy rhythm each evening.
Walks to schoolChildren moving through the village on foot, unhurried.
Tennis at RanelaghAn afternoon match, then a drink overlooking the water.

People know each other here. Familiar faces at the village butcher. The same parents at Saturday sport. It's a place that quietly pulls people out of isolation and back into community.

A genuine village - wealth here expresses itself quietly.

And yet, Mt Eliza has never become a spectacle.

There are extraordinary homes here, certainly. Architect-designed houses hidden behind established trees. Mid-century beach homes held by the same families for decades. Large blocks. Gardens that have had years to settle into themselves.

But wealth in Mt Eliza tends to express itself quietly. People come here for space, privacy and permanence - not visibility.

Increasingly, families are discovering that this kind of life is difficult to recreate.

Not because it is remote. Because places like this are becoming rare.

A genuine village. Established landscape. Excellent schools. A coastline that still feels local. Enough space for children to grow up outdoors and independent. All within reach of Melbourne.

The homes offered here begin with that understanding.

Each has been carefully designed in response to the land itself - the light, the vegetation, the privacy, the way a family might actually want to live day to day.

The most difficult work has already been done. The land is secured. The architecture is resolved. And critically, planning approval has already been obtained - removing the time, risk and uncertainty that this stage usually carries.

What remains is the opportunity to lightly personalise a remarkable home, then move directly to a fixed-price build - without absorbing years of complexity, compromise and decision fatigue along the way.

Because ultimately, this is not simply about moving house. It is about choosing a different rhythm for the years ahead.
